name: Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà — Circular desde el Embalse de Ulldecona hasta la Haya Padre
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Embassament d'Ulldecona traverse diverse terrain within the Tinença de Benifassà and Ports de Tortosa-Beseit mountains. The region features the Ulldecona reservoir, formed by the confluence of seasonal rivers, contrasting with surrounding calcareous rock formations. Mountain bikers can expect varied landscapes, including mid-mountain areas, ravines with high walls, and ancient olive groves. The area offers a robust network of trails suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Embassament d'Ulldecona The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà — Circular desde el Embalse de Ulldecona hasta la Haya Padre, a 37.9 miles (61.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the natural park, offering extensive mountain views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Ulldecona Reservoir – Scenic Mountain Trail loop from Embassament d'Ulldecona, a moderate 16.5 miles (26.6 km) path. This route provides scenic views around the reservoir and through mountainous terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Convento de Benifassà – Ulldecona Reservoir loop from Embassament d'Ulldecona, a 16.1 miles (26.0 km) trail leading through mid-mountain areas, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Mountain biking around Embassament d'Ulldecona is defined by the reservoir, calcareous mountains, and ravines. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Embassament d'Ulldecona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Embassament d'Ulldecona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Embassament d'Ulldecona?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Embassament d'Ulldecona, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes explore the diverse terrain of the Tinença de Benifassà and Ports de Tortosa-Beseit mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Embassament d'Ulldecona?
A: The terrain around Embassament d'Ulldecona is quite varied. You'll find paths traversing mid-mountain areas, ravines with high walls, and ancient olive groves. The region is characterized by calcareous rocks, which add to the unique landscape. Some trails may include steep uphill and downhill sections, offering a mix of challenges.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, Embassament d'Ulldecona offers several easy and family-friendly paths suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride. These trails often provide scenic views without overly technical sections, making them enjoyable for all ages.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints to look out for on the trails?
A: The reservoir itself is a central feature, offering picturesque views where the green of the trees meets the blue-green of the water. You can also expect to encounter high walls and rock shelters in ravines like the Barranc de la Ronyosa. The abandoned village of Mangraner, located at the northwestern end of the reservoir, offers a glimpse into the past, and panoramic views can be enjoyed from spots like the Turó del Molí Nou.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Embassament d'Ulldecona?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38219910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ulldecona Reservoir – Scenic Mountain Trail loop from Embassament d'Ulldecona&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.5-mile loop offering scenic views around the reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38217298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convento de Benifassà – Ulldecona Reservoir loop from Embassament d'Ulldecona&lt;/a&gt;, a 16.1-mile trail through mid-mountain areas.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Embassament d'Ulldecona?
A: The region is suitable for outdoor activities for much of the year. However, the warmer months allow for water activities in the reservoir, which can be a pleasant addition to your trip. For mountain biking, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures and conditions, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Where can I park my car when visiting Embassament d'Ulldecona for mountain biking?
A: Parking is generally available near the Embassament d'Ulldecona, serving as a common starting point for many trails. Specific parking areas are usually indicated at trailheads or popular access points around the reservoir.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Embassament d'Ulldecona?
A: The mountain bike routes in Embassament d'Ulldecona are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from more than 20 reviews. Over 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views and diverse trail options.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The area offers 21 difficult routes for experienced riders. A notable challenging route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931081047/parque-natural-de-la-tinenca-de-benifassa-circular-desde-el-embalse-de-ulldecona-hasta-la-haya-padre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà — Circular desde el Embalse de Ulldecona hasta la Haya Padre&lt;/a&gt;, a 37.9-mile trail with significant elevation gain, exploring the natural park and offering extensive mountain views.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas within the Tinença de Benifassà and Ports de Tortosa-Beseit mountains. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails if kept under control, but some areas might have restrictions or require them to be on a leash.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay near the Embassament d'Ulldecona mountain bike trails?
A: The Embassament d'Ulldecona is located within the municipal limits of La Pobla de Benifassà. While the immediate vicinity of the reservoir might have limited options, nearby towns and villages in the Tinença de Benifassà region will offer cafes, restaurants, and accommodation choices for visitors.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available?
A: The longest mountain bike trail listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931081047/parque-natural-de-la-tinenca-de-benifassà-circular-desde-el-embalse-de-ulldecona-hasta-la-haya-padre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Tinença de Benifassà — Circular desde el Embalse de Ulldecona hasta la Haya Padre&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 37.9 miles (61.0 km) and typically takes nearly 6 hours to complete.
tips: Small town in the interior of Castellón with typical buildings, many of them in ruins and others very well preserved. It has some services although you will only find the basics there.

The Faig pare (Haya padre) has a majestic bare root full of whimsical shapes that makes it one of the most beautiful trees in the Els Ports Natural Park. Declared a monumental tree by the DARP on December 3, 1992, it is located within the partial nature reserve of La Fageda dels Ports. It is approximately 250 years old.
